# flashalpha-historical (JavaScript / TypeScript SDK)

> Official Node.js SDK for the FlashAlpha Historical API ŌĆö
> point-in-time replay of every live analytics endpoint back to
> **2018-04-16**, in the same response shape as the live API. Includes
> a built-in backtester with NYSE holiday calendar (2018-2026) and
> async iterators over date / minute ranges. Node 18+, zero deps.

## What FlashAlpha Historical is

The Historical API mirrors every analytics endpoint on the live
[FlashAlpha](https://flashalpha.com) API but takes an extra `at=`
parameter. Ask "what did SPY's gamma flip / VRP / max pain / 0DTE pin
risk / full stock summary look like at 2020-03-16T15:30 ET?" and get
back the exact same JSON shape as the live endpoint, replayed from
the historical options-and-greeks dataset.

Use cases:

- **Backtesting** options strategies that depend on dealer
  positioning (GEX regime, VRP harvest score, 0DTE pin probability).
- **Event studies** ŌĆö what did dealer positioning look like the
  morning of a Fed announcement, CPI print, or earnings event?
- **Training data generation** for ML models keyed on options-flow
  features.
- **Replay-driven UI demos** ŌĆö scrub a chart's positioning panel
  back through time without standing up your own time-series store.

## What this SDK provides

A single `FlashAlphaHistorical` client class wrapping every analytics
endpoint, plus replay utilities:

- TypeScript interfaces for the major response shapes (rich JSDoc on
  load-bearing fields ŌĆö same shapes as the live SDK)
- `Backtester` class for evaluating a strategy callback over a date
  range against any one endpoint
- `iterDays(start, end)`, `iterMinutes(start, end, {stepMinutes})`,
  `isTradingDay(date)` ŌĆö NYSE-aware async iterators
- `replay(client, method, symbol, iterator)` ŌĆö generic
  AsyncGenerator yielding `{at, response}` for any endpoint
- typed errors (`AuthenticationError`, `TierRestrictedError`,
  `RateLimitError`, `NoCoverageError`, `NoDataError`,
  `InsufficientDataError`, `InvalidAtError`, `SymbolNotFoundError`,
  `ServerError`, `FlashAlphaHistoricalError`)
- Node-native `fetch` and `AbortController`

Quick start:

```ts
import { FlashAlphaHistorical } from 'flashalpha-historical';

const hx = new FlashAlphaHistorical(process.env.FLASHALPHA_API_KEY!);

// What did SPY's dealer positioning look like at the COVID-crash close?
const snap = await hx.exposureSummary('SPY', { at: '2020-03-16T15:30:00' });
console.log(snap.regime, snap.gamma_flip);
```

The `at` argument accepts:

- `'2026-03-05T15:30:00'` ŌĆö minute-level
- `'2026-03-05'` ŌĆö date-only, defaults to 16:00 ET (close)
- `Date` instances ŌĆö formatted using the local clock (ET assumed)

## Key endpoints

Same as the live API (every endpoint also requires `at`):

- `stockSummary(symbol, {at})` ŌĆö single best snapshot (price + IV +
  HV + VRP + skew + term structure + flow + full exposure + macro).
- `exposureSummary(symbol, {at})` ŌĆö regime, GEX/DEX/VEX/CHEX, gamma
  flip, walls, hedging estimate.
- `exposureLevels(symbol, {at})` ŌĆö just the key support/resistance
  strikes (gamma flip, walls, max pos/neg gamma, highest OI, 0DTE
  magnet).
- `narrative(symbol, {at})` ŌĆö LLM-friendly verbal output of the
  exposure block.
- `zeroDte(symbol, {at})` ŌĆö full 0DTE dashboard.
- `vrp(symbol, {at})` ŌĆö variance risk premium dashboard.
- `maxPain(symbol, {at})` ŌĆö max pain strike, pain curve, dealer
  alignment, pin probability.
- `gex` / `dex` / `vex` / `chex` ŌĆö exposure curves by strike.

Replay:

```ts
import { iterMinutes, replay } from 'flashalpha-historical';

for await (const { at, response } of replay(
  hx,
  'exposureSummary',
  'SPY',
  iterMinutes('2025-01-15', '2025-01-15', { stepMinutes: 15 }),
)) {
  console.log(at, response.regime, response.underlying_price);
}
```

## Coverage and snapping

- Coverage starts **2018-04-16**.
- Every analytic snaps `at` to the nearest available minute on or
  before the request ŌĆö always inspect `as_of` on the response to
  confirm the snap.
- NYSE holidays and weekends are skipped silently by `iterDays` /
  `iterMinutes` / `Backtester`.
- Quota: every call counts against the daily plan quota (shared with
  live). A 1-minute replay = 390 calls per analytic per day; use
  `stepMinutes` aggressively in development.

## Try without writing code

- Interactive playground (Swagger UI): https://lab.flashalpha.com/swagger
- Sign up for an API key: https://flashalpha.com

## Tiers

The Historical API requires **Alpha plan or higher** on every endpoint
(it's a power-user product). See https://flashalpha.com for tier
comparison and pricing.

## Companion SDKs

- `flashalpha` ŌĆö the live SDK with the same client surface, no `at=`.
- Python, .NET, Java, Go SDKs at https://github.com/FlashAlpha-lab.
